questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. The CEO took the opportunity to reaffirm the company’s commitment to sustainability during the conference.
questions.explanation: B) Reaffirm(verb) – Confirm, Restate, Reiterate, Assert, Validate पुष्टिकरना Antonym: Deny(verb) – Refuse to accept or acknowledge; reject.इनकार करना Shrink(verb) – Become or make smaller in size or amount. सिकुड़ना Expand(verb) – Become or make larger or more extensive. विस्तारित करना Inflate(verb) – Increase (something) by a large or excessive amount. फुलाना

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group ofwords. A person in charge of the old people’s home or a hostel
questions.explanation: C)Warden(noun) – A person in charge of the old people’s home or a hostel अधीक्षक

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The biggest platforms for budding (A) / singers in today’s age (B) / are reality shows but they’re (C) / a dime in dozen. (D)
questions.explanation: D) 'a dime in dozen'के बदले'a dime a dozen'का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँidiomatic expression errorहै। सही मुहावरा"a dime a dozen"(आम/साधारण वस्तु) है, न कि"a dime in dozen"; जैसे—Talented singers are a dime a dozen in the music industry. •'a dime a dozen'will be used instead of'a dime in dozen'because there is anidiomatic expression error. The correct phrase is"a dime a dozen"(meaning something common or plentiful), not"a dime in dozen"; Like—Talented singers are a dime a dozen in the music industry.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Obsequious
questions.explanation: A)Obsequious(adjective) – Excessively obedient or attentive to please someone, servile, submissive. चापलूस, गुलाम-स्वभाव वाला। Antonym:Obstinate(adjective) – Stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion, headstrong, inflexible. ज़िद्दी, हठी। Ostracised(verb) – Exclude someone from a society or group, shunned, alienated.बहिष्कृत। Obliterate(verb) – Destroy utterly, wipe out, eradicate. नष्ट करना। Ordained(verb) – Decree officially, appoint, consecrate. आध्यात्मिक नियुक्ति।

questions.question: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Select the option that arranges the parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence. (P) unlock their potential (Q) empowers individuals to (O) and shape their future (R) the power of education
questions.explanation: D)RQPO the power of education empowers individuals to unlock their potential and shape their futureR starts the sentence: “the power of education,” which introduces the main subject, focusing on the concept of education’s influence. Q follows “R” with “empowers individuals to,” explaining what the power of education does and whom it impacts. P comes next: “unlock their potential,” specifying the action that individuals are empowered to do, making the idea clearer. O concludes the sentence: “and shape their future,” adding what else education helps individuals achieve, completing the thought comprehensively

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The Indian monsoon has been (A) / progressing slow with rainfall 37% (B) / below average since (C) / the start of the season on June 1. (D)
options: the start of the season on June 1. (D)
questions.explanation: B) 'slow'के बदले'slowly'का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँadverb usage errorहै। Verb"progressing"को modify करने के लिए adverb"slowly"की आवश्यकता है, न कि adjective"slow"की; जैसे—The traffic is moving slowly due to construction work. •'slowly'will be used instead of'slow'because there is anadverb usage error. The verb"progressing"requires the adverb"slowly"to describehowit is progressing, not the adjective"slow"; Like—The traffic is moving slowly due to construction work.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Rain cats and dogs
questions.explanation: C)Rain cats and dogs(idiom)- Raining heavily बारिश बहुत तेज़ी से होना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. She ______ the book to her friend.
questions.explanation: A) ‘Lent'का use होगा क्योंकि यह "lend" का past tense है, जिसका अर्थ है किसी वस्तु को अस्थायी रूप से किसी को देना। sentence में mention है कि उसने किताब अपने दोस्त को दी, इसलिए past tense का use किया जाएगा। 'Leant' का अर्थ है "झुकना," जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। 'Lend' present tense है और यह वाक्य के grammar से मेल नहीं खाता। 'Lent'will be used because it is the past tense of "lend," which means to temporarily give something to someone. The sentence indicates a completed action in the past, so the past tense is appropriate. 'Leant' means "to lean," which is contextually incorrect here. 'Lend' is in the present tense and does not fit the grammatical structure of the sentence.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The medical fraternity (A) / fear that the law (B) / would be adversely (C) / used against it. (D)
questions.explanation: B) 'fear' के बदले 'fears' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि Subject 'The medical fraternity' Singular Collective Noun है जिसके साथ Singular Verb ('fears') आएगी।'fears' will be used instead of 'fear' because the Subject 'The medical fraternity' is a Singular Collective Noun, which takes a Singular Verb ('fears').Subject-Verb Agreement: "The medical fraternity" refers to a single group (collective noun), so it requires a singular verb. Incorrect: "The medical fraternity fear..." (using plural verb 'fear') Correct: "The medical fraternity fears..." (using singular verb 'fears')

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. Surviving the accident was an ordeal that changed his life forever.
questions.explanation: C) Ordeal(noun) - Trauma, Hardship, Trial, Agony, Suffering कठिन परीक्षा Synonym: Agony(noun) – Extreme suffering. (पीड़ा) Pleasure(noun) – Happiness, enjoyment. (आनंद) Joy(noun) – Delight, bliss. (खुशी) Comfort(noun) – Ease, relief. (सुख)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. How long have you been _________ these contact lenses?
questions.explanation: B)'Wearing'का use किया जाएगा क्योंकि "wearing" का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को पहनना या use में लेना। sentence में पूछा गया है कि आप इन कॉन्टैक्ट लेंस को कितने समय से use कर रहे हैं, इसलिए 'wearing' सही है। जबकि 'seeing' का अर्थ है देखना, 'swimming' का अर्थ है तैरना, और 'prevailing' का अर्थ है प्रचलित होना, जो इस संदर्भ में फिट नहीं होते। 'Wearing'will be used because it means to have something on or to use something. The sentence is asking how long the person has been using contact lenses, making 'wearing' the correct option. Whereas, 'seeing' means to look, 'swimming' means to swim, and 'prevailing' means to be widespread, none of which fit in this context.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. An absolute government by one man
questions.explanation: C)Autocracy(noun) – Government in which one person has uncontrolled or unlimited authority over others.तानाशाही

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. It is important to nurture young talent to help them reach their full potential.
questions.explanation: A) Nurture(verb) – Foster, Cultivate, Encourage, Support, Develop पालन–पोषण करना Synonym: Foster(verb) – Promote, encourage development. (प्रोत्साहित करना) Neglect(verb) – Ignore, fail to care for. (उपेक्षा करना) Hinder(verb) – Obstruct, delay. (रोकना) Destroy(verb) – Ruin, demolish. (नष्ट करना)

questions.question: Based on the situation in the sentence, choose the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank. You cannot _________________ before replicating your successes and achievements multiple times; otherwise, people will deem your success a stroke of dumb luck.
questions.explanation: C)Rest on your laurels(idiom) – To stop making an effort to achieve something new and assume that past success is enough for continued recognition. अपनी पिछली उपलब्धियों के सहारे आराम करना और आगे की सफलता के लिए प्रयास न करना। Sit on the fence(idiom) – To avoid making a decision or choice between two options.तटस्थ रहना या किसी पक्ष का समर्थन करने से बचना Cut to the chase(idiom) – To get to the point quickly without wasting time. सीधे मुद्दे पर आना। Play devil's advocate(idiom) – To argue against an idea for the sake of debate, even if one does not personally agree with it. जानबूझकर विरोध करना या चर्चा के लिए विपरीत तर्क देना।

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. At least four people died in (A) / bathing accidents in (B) / different parts in the (C) / country on Wednesday. (D)
options: At least four people died in (A)
questions.explanation: C) 'parts in the'के बदले'parts of the'का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँpreposition errorहै। सही preposition"of"है जो location के संबंध को दर्शाता है, न कि"in"; जैसे—The festival is celebrated in different parts of the world. •'parts of the'will be used instead of'parts in the'because there is apreposition error. The correct preposition is"of"to indicate belonging/relation to locations, not"in"; Like—The festival is celebrated in different parts of the world.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. Solar energy is a viable alternative to fossil fuels for sustainable development.
questions.explanation: A) Viable(adjective) – Feasible, Practical, Workable, Achievable, Sustainable व्यवहार्य Antonym: Impractical(adjective) – Not practical or realistic; not adapted for use or action. अव्यावहारिक Feasible(adjective) – Possible and practical to do easily or conveniently. संभव Achievable(adjective) – Able to be achieved or attained. प्राप्य Practical(adjective) – Concerned with the actual doing or use of something rather than with theory and ideas. व्यावहारिक

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Let the cat out of the bag
options: To buy a cat and a bag
To release a cat out of a bag
To not allow a cat into the bag
questions.explanation: D)Let the cat out of the bag(idiom) – To reveal a secret रहस्य को प्रकट करना

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word
questions.explanation: C) The incorrectly spelt word isMedicin. The correct spelling isMedicine.दवाईयाऔषधि

questions.question: Select the correct spelling of the underlined word. She has a magnificient personality.
questions.explanation: A) The correct spelling of the underlined word ‘magnificient’is ‘magnificent’, which means "extremely beautiful, elaborate, or impressive" (अत्यंत सुंदर, शानदार, भव्य).

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. This decision underscores the importance of fostering an inclusive atmosphere while maintaining discipline and uniformity within institutions. B. The Supreme Court’s decision to partially stay the Mumbai college circular banning the hijab, while upholding restrictions on the niqab and burqa, strikes a commendable balance between respecting religious sentiments and addressing the pragmatic needs of modern education. C. By allowing the hijab but restricting face-covering attire like the niqab and burqa, the SC has set a precedent that other institutions with similar issues would do well to follow. D. It is a nuanced attempt to reconcile cultural traditions with the demands of contemporary academic environment.
questions.explanation: A)BDAC B: This sentence introduces the topic of the Supreme Court’s judgment, which is essential for setting the tone of the paragraph.D: "It" is a pronoun that refers back to the decision in Sentence B. The use of this pronoun links the two sentences, making D the natural continuation.A: This decision" is a demonstrative pronoun that specifically points to the idea discussed in Sentence D, creating cohesion between D and A.C: C concludes the paragraph as it talks about the precedent set by the Supreme Court's decision, which relates to how other institutions might follow this example.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The habit of reading is highly advantageous to us intellectually, emotionally and spiritually. As one gets into the habit of reading, one gets mental (1) ________. It helps us acquire (2) ________and attain intellectual (3)_________. Reading takes away our (4)_________. Reading can bring (5) __________ in our sorrowful times and guide us in our duties. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1
questions.explanation: A) ‘Satisfaction’का use होगा क्योंकि यह मानसिक संतुष्टि या खुशी दर्शाता है, जो पठन-पाठन की आदत से स्वाभाविक रूप से हासिल होती है। जबकि ‘Exhaustion’ का अर्थ थकान है, जो पढ़ने की सकारात्मक प्रक्रिया से मेल नहीं खाता; ‘Coverage’ संदर्भ से बाहर है; और ‘Success’ सीधे मानसिक संतुष्टि या शांति को व्यक्त नहीं करता। ‘Satisfaction’ will be used because it conveys the sense of mental contentment or pleasure naturally gained from reading. Meanwhile, ‘Exhaustion’ means tiredness, which conflicts with the positive nature of reading; ‘Coverage’ is contextually irrelevant; and ‘Success’ does not directly represent mental peace or contentment.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The habit of reading is highly advantageous to us intellectually, emotionally and spiritually. As one gets into the habit of reading, one gets mental (1) ________. It helps us acquire (2) ________and attain intellectual (3)_________. Reading takes away our (4)_________. Reading can bring (5) __________ in our sorrowful times and guide us in our duties. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: B)'Knowledge'का उपयोग होगा क्योंकि knowledge का अर्थ है जानकारी या ज्ञान जो किसी विषय के बारे में प्राप्त की जाती है। यहाँ पाठ में कहा गया है कि पढ़ने की आदत से हम "ज्ञान प्राप्त" करते हैं, जो सही रूप में intellectual development का हिस्सा है। 'Strategies' का अर्थ है योजनाएँ या रणनीतियाँ, जो यहाँ ज्ञान प्राप्त करने के संदर्भ में फिट नहीं होता। 'Readiness' का अर्थ है तैयार रहना, जो भी sentence में contextually सही नहीं है।'Employment' का अर्थ है नौकरी या कार्य, जो इस intellectual और emotional development से संबंधित विषय में inappropriate है। 'Knowledge'will be used because knowledge means information or understanding gained through learning or experience. The passage discusses the benefits of reading, and acquiring "knowledge" perfectly fits the context of intellectual growth. 'Strategies' means plans or methods, which do not fit the idea of gaining intellectual development through reading. 'Readiness' means preparedness, which does not align with the context of acquiring something through reading. 'Employment' means a job or work, which is unrelated to the intellectual and emotional benefits mentioned in the passage.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The habit of reading is highly advantageous to us intellectually, emotionally and spiritually. As one gets into the habit of reading, one gets mental (1) ________. It helps us acquire (2) ________and attain intellectual (3)_________. Reading takes away our (4)_________. Reading can bring (5) __________ in our sorrowful times and guide us in our duties. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: D)'Expansion'का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है विस्तार या किसी चीज़ का बढ़ना। वाक्य में कहा गया है कि पढ़ने की आदत हमें ज्ञान प्राप्त करने और बौद्धिक विकास (intellectual growth) में मदद करती है। 'Expansion' का अर्थ बौद्धिक विकास के साथ मेल खाता है। 'Compression' का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को घटाना या संकुचित करना, जो यहाँ सही नहीं है। 'Stagnation' का अर्थ है ठहराव या प्रगति की कमी, जो वाक्य के सकारात्मक अर्थ के विरुद्ध है। 'Creation' का अर्थ है नई चीज़ का निर्माण, जो बौद्धिक विकास के संदर्भ में उपयुक्त नहीं है। Expansion'will be used because it refers to the act of increasing in size, scope, or intellectual capacity. The sentence discusses how reading helps us acquire knowledge and achieve intellectual growth, which directly aligns with the meaning of "expansion." Compression: It means reducing the size or volume, which contradicts the idea of intellectual growth. Stagnation: It means a lack of growth or progress, which is opposite to the positive impact of reading mentioned here. Creation: It refers to producing something new, but it does not align with the concept of intellectual "growth" as implied in the sentence.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The habit of reading is highly advantageous to us intellectually, emotionally and spiritually. As one gets into the habit of reading, one gets mental (1) ________. It helps us acquire (2) ________and attain intellectual (3)_________. Reading takes away our (4)_________. Reading can bring (5) __________ in our sorrowful times and guide us in our duties. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: A)'Boredom'का उपयोग सही है क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है उबाऊपन या नीरसता। यहाँ वाक्य में कहा गया है कि "Reading takes away our..." यानी कि पढ़ने से हमारी कौन-सी चीज़ दूर होती है? जब कोई पढ़ता है, तो यह उसे बोरियत (boredom) से बाहर निकालता है। Time (समय) सही नहीं है क्योंकि पढ़ाई समय को दूर नहीं करती, बल्कि उसे सार्थक बनाती है। Vigour (उत्साह) भी गलत है, क्योंकि पढ़ाई उत्साह को दूर नहीं करती बल्कि उसे बढ़ाती है। Concern (चिंता) गलत है क्योंकि पढ़ाई चिंता को खत्म करने का उल्लेख यहाँ नहीं कर रही 'Boredom'is the correct choice because it means a state of dullness or lack of interest. The sentence states that "Reading takes away our...", referring to something negative that reading alleviates. Reading keeps the mind engaged and prevents a feeling of dullness or boredom. Time is incorrect because reading doesn’t take away time; instead, it utilizes it meaningfully. Vigour is wrong because reading doesn’t take away energy; it can enhance or stimulate it. Concern is inappropriate because the sentence doesn’t suggest reading eliminates worries or concerns directly.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The habit of reading is highly advantageous to us intellectually, emotionally and spiritually. As one gets into the habit of reading, one gets mental (1) ________. It helps us acquire (2) ________and attain intellectual (3)_________. Reading takes away our (4)_________. Reading can bring (5) __________ in our sorrowful times and guide us in our duties. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: B)Comfort'का चयन किया गया है क्योंकि "comfort" का अर्थ है सांत्वना या राहत देना, विशेषकर कठिन समय में। वाक्य में कहा गया है कि reading हमारे दुःख भरे समय में राहत ला सकती है और हमारे कर्तव्यों में हमारा मार्गदर्शन कर सकती है। इसलिए 'comfort' यहाँ सटीक और संदर्भानुकूल है। Resentment (नाराजगी): इसका अर्थ है किसी के प्रति नकारात्मक भावना या गुस्सा। यह संदर्भ के अनुसार सटीक नहीं है क्योंकि यहाँ पढ़ने के सकारात्मक प्रभाव की बात हो रही है। Oppression (दमन): इसका अर्थ है दबाव या अत्याचार। यह शब्द यहाँ विपरीत अर्थ देता है और संदर्भ में फिट नहीं होता। Lethargy (सुस्ती): इसका अर्थ है आलस्य या निष्क्रियता। पढ़ना सुस्ती दूर करता है, न कि बढ़ाता है, इसलिए यह संदर्भ के अनुरूप नहीं है Comfort'is the correct choice because it means solace or relief, especially during difficult or sorrowful times. The sentence states that reading can bring relief during our sorrowful times and guide us in our duties, which makes 'comfort' the most appropriate option. Resentment: This means bitterness or anger towards someone. It does not fit here as the sentence talks about the positive effects of reading. Oppression: This means cruelty or heavy pressure, which conveys a negative meaning and does not align with the context. Lethargy: This means laziness or inactivity. Reading is generally associated with reducing lethargy, not increasing it, so it does not fit here.
